description: Pothole detected on road surface via AI Vision analysis fallback classification.
procedure: 1. Establish a safe work zone by placing traffic cones, barricades, and warning signs at least 50 feet in all directions from the pothole. Use flaggers if on a high-traffic road.
2. Assess the pothole dimensions and depth to determine material quantities. Mark a square around the pothole extending 6-12 inches beyond edges for clean repair boundaries.
3. Clean the pothole thoroughly: remove all water, debris, and loose material using a broom, compressed air blower, or vacuum. Ensure the hole is dry.
4. Cut vertical edges around the pothole using a concrete saw or pavement cutter to create a square or rectangular shape with clean, square edges at least 2-3 inches deep.
5. Apply a tack coat (emulsified asphalt) to the vertical edges and bottom of the hole to ensure good bonding. Allow it to set briefly.
6. Fill the pothole with hot mix asphalt (HMA) in 2-3 inch lifts, starting from the bottom. Overfill slightly to account for compaction.
7. Compact each lift thoroughly using a vibrating plate compactor or roller until no further settlement occurs. Achieve at least 95% density.
8. Apply a final thin layer of HMA if needed and compact to match the surrounding road surface level and texture.
9. Seal the repair edges with crack sealant if necessary to prevent water infiltration.
10. Clean the work area, remove all traffic control devices, and inspect the repair for smoothness and safety.
materials_required: Broom, compressed air blower, or vacuum
Hot mix asphalt (HMA), quantity based on pothole volume (e.g., 1-2 tons for large HIGH severity pothole)
Personal Protective Equipment (PPE: gloves, safety vests, hard hats, eye protection)
Cleaning supplies (rags, absorbent material for spills)

description: Too much garbage
procedure: 1. Don personal protective equipment (PPE) including gloves, safety vest, and boots to ensure personal safety.
2. Assess the accumulation site to determine the volume, type of waste (e.g., organic, recyclable, hazardous), and any immediate hazards.
3. Segregate waste if possible: separate recyclables, organics, and general waste.
4. Use appropriate tools to collect garbage into heavy-duty trash bags or bins, starting from the edges and working inward.
5. Securely tie bags and load them onto a cart, wheelbarrow, or vehicle for transport.
6. Transport collected waste to the designated municipal waste collection point, dumpster, or landfill in compliance with local regulations.
7. Return to the site and clean the area using brooms, hoses, or pressure washers with mild detergent to remove residues.
8. Disinfect surfaces if necessary, especially in high-traffic sanitation areas.
9. Inspect the area for completeness of cleanup and take photos for records.
10. Report the completion via maintenance log or app, noting any recurring issues for preventive measures.

description: Persistent garbage near Powai Lake
procedure: 1. Conduct site assessment: Survey the garbage accumulation near Powai Lake, estimate volume and types of waste (e.g., plastic, organic, hazardous), identify hazards like sharp objects or contaminated water, and mark the perimeter with safety cones or tape.

2. Assemble and brief team: Gather trained cleanup crew (minimum 10-15 personnel for high severity), provide PPE briefing, assign roles (collectors, sorters, transporters), and ensure compliance with local regulations (e.g., BMC guidelines for Mumbai).
3. Segregate waste: Categorize garbage into recyclables, biodegradables, non-recyclables, and hazardous materials using designated bins or areas to facilitate proper disposal.
4. Collect and bag waste: Use gloves and tools to pick up small debris into heavy-duty bags; employ rakes and shovels for bulk waste; use nets or poles for lake-edge or floating garbage.

5. Load and transport: Transfer bagged and bulk waste to wheelbarrows or carts, then load onto trucks for transport to authorized municipal landfill or recycling center (coordinate with local waste management authority).
6. Clean and disinfect site: Sweep remaining debris, apply eco-friendly disinfectants if needed, and rinse accessible areas with water (avoid lake contamination).

7. Verify and report: Inspect site for completeness, take before/after photos, document waste quantities, and submit report to relevant authorities (e.g., BMC, lake conservation committee). Schedule follow-up monitoring to prevent recurrence.
materials_required: Safety boots and high-visibility vests (for team)
Shovels, rakes, and litter grabbers (20 sets)
Safety cones, caution tape, and signage
Nets and poles for water-edge cleanup
